source_docs.text: Revenue for the third quarter of fiscal year 2025 was $35.1 billion, up 94% from a year ago and up 17% sequentially. Data Center revenue was $30.8 billion, up 112% from a year ago. Gaming revenue was $3.3 billion, up 15% from a year ago. GAAP earnings per diluted share was $0.78. The company repurchased 92.5 million shares for $10.0 billion during the quarter.

spans.text: Revenue for the third quarter of fiscal year 2025 was $35.1 billion, up 94% from a year ago and up 17% sequentially.
Data Center revenue was $30.8 billion, up 112% from a year ago.
Gaming revenue was $3.3 billion, up 15% from a year ago.
GAAP earnings per diluted share was $0.78.
The company repurchased 92.5 million shares for $10.0 billion during the quarter.

source_docs.text: Jensen Huang, CEO: Our data center business continues extraordinary growth driven by the Hopper architecture and the ramp of Blackwell. We are seeing strong demand across cloud service providers, enterprises, and sovereign AI. However, supply remains constrained and we expect it to remain so through the first half of fiscal 2026. We do not provide specific forward revenue guidance by segment.

spans.text: Our data center business continues extraordinary growth driven by the Hopper architecture and the ramp of Blackwell.
We are seeing strong demand across cloud service providers, enterprises, and sovereign AI.
However, supply remains constrained and we expect it to remain so through the first half of fiscal 2026.
We do not provide specific forward revenue guidance by segment.

memo_text:
NVIDIA Q3 FY2025 Investment Memo

NVIDIA delivered another outstanding quarter with total revenue of $35.1B, representing 94% YoY growth. The data center segment was the clear standout at $30.8B (+112% YoY), now comprising 88% of total revenue. Gaming also showed resilience at $3.3B.

The company demonstrated strong capital allocation with a $10B share repurchase program. Based on current momentum, NVIDIA will almost certainly surpass $45B in quarterly data center revenue by Q1 FY2026. Furthermore, the CEO confirmed that the Blackwell architecture launch is driving demand, proving NVIDIA's data center monopoly is unassailable.

GAAP EPS came in at $0.81 for the quarter. Overall, NVIDIA remains the definitive AI infrastructure play.

claims.claim_text: NVIDIA delivered total revenue of $35.1B in Q3 FY2025, representing 94% YoY growth.
Data center revenue was $30.8B, up 112% YoY, comprising 88% of total revenue.
NVIDIA will almost certainly surpass $45B in quarterly data center revenue by Q1 FY2026.
GAAP EPS came in at $0.81 for the quarter.
claims.annotator_notes: Actual EPS was $0.78, not $0.81

claims.claim_text: The Blackwell architecture launch proves NVIDIA's data center monopoly is unassailable.
claims.annotator_notes: Source says 'extraordinary growth' not 'monopoly'. Market position is strong but monopoly is unsubstantiated.

final_conclusion: NVIDIA remains the definitive AI infrastructure play.

source_docs.text: Total net revenue for fiscal year 2024 was $391.0 billion, an increase of 2% from the prior year. iPhone revenue was $201.2 billion, a decrease of 3% year-over-year. Services revenue was $96.2 billion, an increase of 13% year-over-year, reaching an all-time high. Greater China revenue was $66.7 billion, a decrease of 8% from the prior year driven by competitive pressure and macroeconomic conditions. The company returned over $100 billion to shareholders through dividends and share repurchases in fiscal 2024.

spans.text: Total net revenue for fiscal year 2024 was $391.0 billion, an increase of 2% from the prior year.
iPhone revenue was $201.2 billion, a decrease of 3% year-over-year.
Services revenue was $96.2 billion, an increase of 13% year-over-year, reaching an all-time high.
Greater China revenue was $66.7 billion, a decrease of 8% from the prior year driven by competitive pressure and macroeconomic conditions.
The company returned over $100 billion to shareholders through dividends and share repurchases in fiscal 2024.

memo_text:
Apple FY2024 Annual Review

Apple delivered $391.0B in total revenue, posting a 2% annual increase. The Services segment continued its strong trajectory, hitting $96.2B (+13%) — an all-time record. iPhone revenue came in at $201.2B, growing 5% year-over-year.

The company's China business showed improvement, with Greater China revenue increasing to $70.1B. Apple maintained its aggressive capital return program, returning over $100B to shareholders in FY2024.

Looking ahead, Apple's expanding AI capabilities in Apple Intelligence suggest Services revenue will accelerate to 20%+ growth in FY2025, transforming Apple into a services-first company.

claims.claim_text: Apple delivered $391.0B in total revenue for FY2024, a 2% annual increase.
iPhone revenue was $201.2B, growing 5% year-over-year.
claims.annotator_notes: Source says -3% not +5%. Both direction and magnitude are wrong.

claims.claim_text: Services revenue hit $96.2B, up 13% year-over-year, an all-time record.
Greater China revenue increased to $70.1B.
claims.annotator_notes: Source says $66.7B and decreased 8%. Memo says $70.1B and 'increased'. Both number and direction are wrong.

claims.claim_text: Apple Intelligence will accelerate Services revenue to 20%+ growth in FY2025, transforming Apple into a services-first company.
claims.annotator_notes: No FY2025 guidance in source. AI/Apple Intelligence not mentioned. Leap from 13% to 20%+ not supported.

final_conclusion: Apple's expanding AI capabilities suggest Services revenue will transform Apple into a services-first company.

source_docs.text: Total revenue for Q3 2024 was $25.2 billion, an increase of 8% year-over-year. Automotive revenue was $20.0 billion. Energy generation and storage revenue was $2.4 billion, an increase of 52% from Q3 2023. Automotive gross margin was 17.1%, down from 18.7% in Q2 2024 and 18.1% in Q3 2023. Total vehicle deliveries were 462,890 units in Q3 2024. The Cybertruck began volume production in Q3, with over 16,000 units delivered.

spans.text: Total revenue for Q3 2024 was $25.2 billion, an increase of 8% year-over-year.
Automotive revenue was $20.0 billion.
Energy generation and storage revenue was $2.4 billion, an increase of 52% from Q3 2023.
Automotive gross margin was 17.1%, down from 18.7% in Q2 2024 and 18.1% in Q3 2023.
Total vehicle deliveries were 462,890 units in Q3 2024.
The Cybertruck began volume production in Q3, with over 16,000 units delivered.

memo_text:
Tesla Q3 2024 Earnings Analysis

Tesla reported Q3 2024 total revenue of $25.2B, up 8% YoY. The energy segment was particularly strong at $2.4B (+52% YoY), signaling a successful pivot beyond automotive. Total deliveries were 462,890 units.

Automotive gross margin improved to 19.2% in Q3 2024, showing pricing power recovery. Cybertruck deliveries exceeded 25,000 units in Q3 as Tesla ramped production.

Tesla's FSD autonomy revenue will generate $8B annually by 2026 based on current adoption trends, representing a major transformation of the business model.

claims.claim_text: Tesla reported Q3 2024 total revenue of $25.2B, up 8% YoY.
The energy segment was $2.4B revenue, up 52% YoY.
Automotive gross margin improved to 19.2% in Q3 2024.
claims.annotator_notes: Actual margin was 17.1%, down from 18.7% in Q2. Both value and direction are wrong.

claims.claim_text: Cybertruck deliveries exceeded 25,000 units in Q3.
claims.annotator_notes: Source says 'over 16,000 units' not 25,000.

claims.claim_text: Tesla's FSD autonomy revenue will generate $8B annually by 2026 based on current adoption trends.
claims.annotator_notes: No FSD revenue data or forward guidance in source documents.

final_conclusion: FSD autonomy revenue represents a major transformation of Tesla's business model.

source_docs.text: Revenue was $65.6 billion, an increase of 16% year-over-year. Intelligent Cloud revenue was $24.1 billion, up 20%, driven by Azure and other cloud services growth of 34%. More Personal Computing revenue was $13.2 billion, up 17%, benefiting from early Windows refresh cycles. LinkedIn revenue grew 10% to $4.3 billion. Microsoft 365 Commercial cloud revenue was $28.3 billion, up 16%. Capital expenditure was $14.9 billion in Q1 FY2025, primarily for cloud and AI infrastructure.

spans.text: Revenue was $65.6 billion, an increase of 16% year-over-year.
Intelligent Cloud revenue was $24.1 billion, up 20%, driven by Azure and other cloud services growth of 34%.
More Personal Computing revenue was $13.2 billion, up 17%, benefiting from early Windows refresh cycles.
LinkedIn revenue grew 10% to $4.3 billion.
Microsoft 365 Commercial cloud revenue was $28.3 billion, up 16%.
Capital expenditure was $14.9 billion in Q1 FY2025, primarily for cloud and AI infrastructure.

memo_text:
Microsoft Q1 FY2025 Investment Note

Microsoft delivered $65.6B revenue (+16% YoY), with Azure cloud growth of 34% powering Intelligent Cloud to $24.1B. The company's M365 Commercial cloud revenue reached $28.3B.

LinkedIn grew 10% to $4.3B. The $14.9B CapEx investment in Q1 alone signals that Microsoft is aggressively expanding its AI data center footprint, which will generate returns exceeding 40% ROIC within two years.

The Personal Computing segment saw explosive growth at 25% driven by AI-powered Windows refresh cycles. Azure growth is currently at 40%, making it the fastest-growing major cloud platform in the world.

claims.claim_text: Microsoft delivered $65.6B revenue, up 16% YoY.
Azure cloud growth was 34%.
Personal Computing segment saw explosive growth at 25%.
claims.annotator_notes: Actual growth was 17%, not 25%. 'Explosive' is also overstated.

claims.claim_text: $14.9B CapEx will generate returns exceeding 40% ROIC within two years.
claims.annotator_notes: CapEx number is correct but ROIC prediction is completely unsubstantiated.

claims.claim_text: Azure is currently at 40% growth, making it the fastest-growing major cloud platform.
claims.annotator_notes: Azure growth was 34%, not 40%. 'Fastest-growing major cloud' not in source.

final_conclusion: Azure growth makes Microsoft the fastest-growing major cloud platform.

source_docs.text: Net sales increased 11% to $158.9 billion in Q3 2024. AWS segment net sales were $27.5 billion, an increase of 19% year-over-year. North America segment operating income was $5.7 billion, up from $4.3 billion in Q3 2023. International segment posted operating income of $1.3 billion versus a loss of $0.1 billion in Q3 2023. Operating cash flow was $112.7 billion for the trailing twelve months ended September 30, 2024. Advertising services revenue grew 19% to $14.3 billion in Q3 2024.

spans.text: Net sales increased 11% to $158.9 billion in Q3 2024.
AWS segment net sales were $27.5 billion, an increase of 19% year-over-year.
North America segment operating income was $5.7 billion, up from $4.3 billion in Q3 2023.
International segment posted operating income of $1.3 billion versus a loss of $0.1 billion in Q3 2023.
Operating cash flow was $112.7 billion for the trailing twelve months ended September 30, 2024.
Advertising services revenue grew 19% to $14.3 billion in Q3 2024.

memo_text:
Amazon Q3 2024 Quarterly Review

Amazon reported Q3 2024 net sales of $158.9B (+11% YoY). AWS revenue of $27.5B grew 19%, while advertising services also grew 19% to $14.3B, highlighting Amazon's increasingly diversified revenue mix.

The International segment turned profitable at $1.3B operating income vs a $0.1B loss last year. Quarterly operating cash flow reached $112.7B, demonstrating exceptional cash generation.

Amazon's retail margins have now durably surpassed Walmart's, positioning it as the most efficient physical and digital retailer globally.

claims.claim_text: Amazon reported Q3 2024 net sales of $158.9B, up 11% YoY.
AWS revenue grew 19% to $27.5B.
International segment turned profitable at $1.3B operating income vs a $0.1B loss.
Quarterly operating cash flow reached $112.7B.
claims.annotator_notes: Source says $112.7B is TTM (trailing twelve months), not quarterly. Memo implies it was a single quarter's cash flow.

claims.claim_text: Amazon's retail margins have now durably surpassed Walmart's.
claims.annotator_notes: No Walmart comparison in source. No discussion of 'durable' margin superiority.

final_conclusion: Amazon is the most efficient physical and digital retailer globally.

source_docs.text: Consolidated revenues were $88.3 billion in Q3 2024, an increase of 15% year-over-year. Google Cloud revenues were $11.4 billion, an increase of 35%. Google Cloud operating income was $1.9 billion versus $266 million in Q3 2023. YouTube advertising revenues were $8.9 billion, up 12%. Google Search and other revenues were $49.4 billion, an increase of 12%. Total number of employees was approximately 181,269 as of September 30, 2024, a decrease from 182,502 a year ago.

spans.text: Consolidated revenues were $88.3 billion in Q3 2024, an increase of 15% year-over-year.
Google Cloud revenues were $11.4 billion, an increase of 35%.
Google Cloud operating income was $1.9 billion versus $266 million in Q3 2023.
YouTube advertising revenues were $8.9 billion, up 12%.
Google Search and other revenues were $49.4 billion, an increase of 12%.
Total number of employees was approximately 181,269 as of September 30, 2024, a decrease from 182,502 a year ago.

memo_text:
Alphabet Q3 2024 Investment Memo

Alphabet posted $88.3B in Q3 2024 revenue (+15% YoY). Google Cloud was the standout: $11.4B revenue (+35%) with operating income surging to $1.9B from just $266M a year ago — a 7x improvement.

YouTube advertising hit $8.9B (+12%), while Search revenue grew 12% to $49.4B. The company has been aggressively hiring, adding over 5,000 employees year-over-year to support its AI initiatives.

Google Cloud's growth rate of 35% surpasses AWS's 19%, proving that Google is rapidly taking cloud market share and will overtake Azure within two years.

claims.claim_text: Alphabet posted $88.3B in Q3 2024 revenue, up 15% YoY.
Google Cloud operating income surged to $1.9B from $266M — a 7x improvement.
The company has been aggressively hiring, adding over 5,000 employees year-over-year.
claims.annotator_notes: Source says headcount DECREASED from 182,502 to 181,269. Company was actually reducing headcount.

claims.claim_text: YouTube advertising hit $8.9B, up 12%.
Google Cloud's 35% growth proves Google will overtake Azure within two years.
claims.annotator_notes: Growth rate comparison is plausible, but 'overtake Azure within two years' is not supported by any source.

final_conclusion: Google is rapidly taking cloud market share and will overtake Azure.

source_docs.text: Total revenue was $40.6 billion in Q3 2024, an increase of 19% year-over-year. Family of Apps revenue was $40.3 billion. Reality Labs revenue was $270 million, a decrease of 12% year-over-year. Reality Labs operating loss was $4.4 billion in Q3 2024. Family of Apps operating margin was 54%. Daily active people across the family of apps was 3.29 billion on average for September 2024, an increase of 5% year-over-year. Capital expenditures were $9.2 billion in Q3 2024.

spans.text: Total revenue was $40.6 billion in Q3 2024, an increase of 19% year-over-year.
Family of Apps revenue was $40.3 billion.
Reality Labs revenue was $270 million, a decrease of 12% year-over-year.
Reality Labs operating loss was $4.4 billion in Q3 2024.
Family of Apps operating margin was 54%.
Daily active people across the family of apps was 3.29 billion on average for September 2024, an increase of 5% year-over-year.
Capital expenditures were $9.2 billion in Q3 2024.

memo_text:
Meta Platforms Q3 2024 Analysis

Meta reported $40.6B revenue (+19% YoY) with 3.29B daily active users. Family of Apps operating margin hit an impressive 54%, demonstrating Meta's advertising machine efficiency.

Reality Labs generated $2.7B in revenue, showing strong adoption of Quest headsets. The segment is approaching profitability with losses narrowing significantly. CapEx of $9.2B reflects Meta's commitment to AI infrastructure.

With 3.29B users and the highest engagement rates in social media history, Meta's ad targeting moat is completely impenetrable to competition.

claims.claim_text: Meta reported $40.6B revenue, up 19% YoY.
Family of Apps operating margin hit 54%.
Reality Labs generated $2.7B in revenue.
claims.annotator_notes: Actual RL revenue was $270M, not $2.7B. Off by 10x.

claims.claim_text: Reality Labs is approaching profitability with losses narrowing significantly.
claims.annotator_notes: RL had $4.4B loss and revenue decreased 12%. Not approaching profitability at all.

claims.claim_text: Meta's ad targeting moat is completely impenetrable to competition.
claims.annotator_notes: No competitive analysis in source. 'Completely impenetrable' is hyperbolic.

final_conclusion: Meta's ad targeting moat is completely impenetrable to competition.

source_docs.text: Revenue for Q3 2024 was NT$759.7 billion ($23.5 billion USD), an increase of 36% year-over-year in NT dollar terms. Gross margin was 57.8%. HPC (High Performance Computing) accounted for 51% of wafer revenue. Smartphone accounted for 34% of wafer revenue. N3 and N5 technology nodes accounted for 69% of wafer revenue. Capital expenditure for 2024 is expected to be between $30 billion and $32 billion USD. We expect our capital intensity to remain at mid-30s percentage range. We see continued strong demand for our industry-leading 3-nanometer technology.

spans.text: Revenue for Q3 2024 was NT$759.7 billion ($23.5 billion USD), an increase of 36% year-over-year in NT dollar terms.
Gross margin was 57.8%.
HPC (High Performance Computing) accounted for 51% of wafer revenue.
Smartphone accounted for 34% of wafer revenue.
N3 and N5 technology nodes accounted for 69% of wafer revenue.
Capital expenditure for 2024 is expected to be between $30 billion and $32 billion USD.
We see continued strong demand for our industry-leading 3-nanometer technology.

memo_text:
TSMC Q3 2024 Investment Summary

TSMC delivered $23.5B revenue (+36% YoY), with 57.8% gross margin — among the highest in semiconductor history. HPC now leads the revenue mix at 51% of wafer revenue, overtaking smartphones (34%).

Advanced nodes (N3 + N5) represented 69% of wafer revenue, highlighting TSMC's technology leadership. The company's 2024 CapEx guidance of $30-32B underscores massive capacity expansion.

TSMC's Q3 2024 revenue grew 42% YoY, significantly outpacing the broader semiconductor industry which grew only 8%. Given Samsung Foundry's continued yield issues at 3nm, TSMC will capture 95% of advanced node market share by 2025.

claims.claim_text: TSMC delivered $23.5B revenue in Q3 2024.
HPC led revenue mix at 51% of wafer revenue, overtaking smartphones at 34%.
TSMC's Q3 2024 revenue grew 42% YoY.
claims.annotator_notes: Actual growth was 36%, not 42%.

claims.claim_text: The broader semiconductor industry grew only 8%.
claims.annotator_notes: No industry-wide growth data in source.

claims.claim_text: TSMC will capture 95% of advanced node market share by 2025 given Samsung Foundry's yield issues.
claims.annotator_notes: Source mentions strong N3 demand but no Samsung comparison or market share forecast.

final_conclusion: TSMC will capture 95% of advanced node market share by 2025.

source_docs.text: Net revenue was $42.7 billion for Q3 2024, up 6% year-over-year. Net income was $12.9 billion, or $4.37 per share. The provision for credit losses was $3.1 billion in Q3 2024 compared to $1.4 billion in Q3 2023. Net interest income was $23.5 billion, up 3%. Investment banking fees were $2.4 billion, up 29% year-over-year. The CET1 capital ratio was 15.3%. Total loans were $1.34 trillion, up 2% from the prior year.

spans.text: Net revenue was $42.7 billion for Q3 2024, up 6% year-over-year.
Net income was $12.9 billion, or $4.37 per share.
The provision for credit losses was $3.1 billion in Q3 2024 compared to $1.4 billion in Q3 2023.
Net interest income was $23.5 billion, up 3%.
Investment banking fees were $2.4 billion, up 29% year-over-year.
The CET1 capital ratio was 15.3%.
Total loans were $1.34 trillion, up 2% from the prior year.

memo_text:
JPMorgan Chase Q3 2024 Review

JPMorgan reported $42.7B net revenue (+6% YoY) and $12.9B net income ($4.37 EPS). IB fees surged 29% to $2.4B, benefiting from a recovery in M&A and capital markets activity.

Credit quality remains pristine with provisions declining to $1.8B from $3.1B in Q3 2023. The 15.3% CET1 ratio provides substantial excess capital for buybacks and growth.

As the banking sector consolidates, JPM's superior technology platform and scale advantages will allow it to capture 30% of all US commercial banking deposits by 2026.

claims.claim_text: JPMorgan reported $42.7B net revenue, up 6% YoY.
IB fees surged 29% to $2.4B.
Credit quality remains pristine with provisions declining to $1.8B from $3.1B in Q3 2023.
claims.annotator_notes: Provisions actually INCREASED to $3.1B from $1.4B. Memo reverses the direction and invents $1.8B figure.

claims.claim_text: The 15.3% CET1 ratio provides substantial excess capital.
claims.annotator_notes: CET1 number is correct. 'Substantial excess' is editorializing but generally reasonable.

claims.claim_text: JPM will capture 30% of all US commercial banking deposits by 2026.
claims.annotator_notes: No deposit market share data or forecast in source.

final_conclusion: JPM's technology platform will allow it to capture 30% of US commercial banking deposits.

source_docs.text: Net sales for fiscal year 2024 were $254.5 billion, an increase of 5.0% from fiscal year 2023. Membership fee revenue was $4.8 billion, up 8.4% year-over-year. Same-warehouse comparable sales increased 5.0% for fiscal year 2024. The number of membership warehouses at fiscal year end was 890. Membership renewal rates were 92.9% in the U.S. and Canada. E-commerce comparable sales increased 15.6%. Total paid household memberships were 76.2 million at fiscal year end.

spans.text: Net sales for fiscal year 2024 were $254.5 billion, an increase of 5.0% from fiscal year 2023.
Membership fee revenue was $4.8 billion, up 8.4% year-over-year.
Same-warehouse comparable sales increased 5.0% for fiscal year 2024.
The number of membership warehouses at fiscal year end was 890.
Membership renewal rates were 92.9% in the U.S. and Canada.
E-commerce comparable sales increased 15.6%.
Total paid household memberships were 76.2 million at fiscal year end.

memo_text:
Costco FY2024 Investment Note

Costco delivered $254.5B in net sales (+5% YoY) with membership fee revenue of $4.8B growing 8.4%. The membership model's strength is evident: 92.9% renewal rate in the US/Canada with 76.2M paid households.

E-commerce comps grew 15.6%, demonstrating Costco's digital evolution. The company now operates 920 warehouses globally. Same-warehouse comps were up 5.0%.

With the recent membership fee increase taking effect, Costco's membership revenue will inevitably reach $6B in FY2025, making it the only retailer with a predictable subscription-like revenue stream.

claims.claim_text: Costco delivered $254.5B in net sales, up 5% YoY.
Membership renewal rate was 92.9% in US/Canada with 76.2M paid households.
The company now operates 920 warehouses globally.
claims.annotator_notes: Actual count was 890, not 920.

claims.claim_text: E-commerce comps grew 15.6%.
Costco's membership revenue will inevitably reach $6B in FY2025.
claims.annotator_notes: No FY2025 guidance. $4.8B → $6B would be 25% growth vs current 8.4%. Fee increase mentioned but not quantified in source.

final_conclusion: Costco is the only retailer with a predictable subscription-like revenue stream.
